sub_mechanisms: M_1: quadratic cost separation in RLHF reward
M_2: Gaussian noise channel as adversarial perturbation
M_3: dual feedback gain over reward-model uncertainty
M_4: co-evolution loop alternating estimator + controller
stripped_llm_application: RLHF expressed as a dual-control loop with a quadratic reward cost and a Gaussian-noise channel on the reward model; the controller (policy) and estimator (reward model) alternate updates under the separation principle, with a feedback gain that absorbs the adversarial noise.
novelty_claim_stripped: Standard RLHF/DPO/PPO + adversarial preference learning (APL 2505.24369) treat reward and policy as coupled but not as LQG separation-principle dual. No paper uses LQG separation explicitly for RLHF.
